task_completion.description: Zotero application is open and displaying the library items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero 7 GUI is visible on screen, showing a list of academic papers (e.g., machine learning papers by LeCun, Krizhevsky, Vaswani) in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: A tag has been successfully applied to at least one item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand item details pane, the 'Tags' tab shows at least one tag added to the selected paper, or a colored tag indicator is visible next to an item in the main list.

task_completion.description: Tags have been applied to multiple items in the library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent selecting different papers and adding tags to them, or the tag selector pane (bottom left) shows tags that filter multiple items when selected.

task_completion.description: At least three distinct tags have been created and applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag selector pane in the bottom-left corner of the Zotero window displays at least three different user-created tags.

task_completion.description: The applied tags are meaningful and based on the research topics of the items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tags visible in the Zotero interface are descriptive of the papers' content (e.g., 'deep learning', 'computer vision', 'NLP', 'attention') rather than generic placeholders (e.g., 'tag1', 'test', 'a').

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to create and apply tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking the 'Tags' tab, typing in the tag input field, or using the Zotero interface, rather than executing SQLite commands in a terminal to modify zotero.sqlite directly.

integrity.description: Tags are applied within the actual Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tags appear natively within the Zotero application's tag pane and item details pane, not just written in a text editor or terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the topic requirement by spamming generic tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tags are not sequential generic words (like 'tag1', 'tag2', 'tag3') or random keystrokes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Zotero library contains 8 real machine learning papers (e.g., by LeCun, Krizhevsky, Vaswani). The agent must create and apply at least 3 distinct tags. Tags must be meaningful (>2 characters, containing letters, not generic like 'tag1' or 'test') and reflect the research topics of the papers (e.g., 'deep learning', 'computer vision', 'NLP'). The verifier expects a minimum of 2 different items to be tagged.
